source_name: Code4rena Nudge.xyz Findings and Analysis Report (2025-03) - Low Risk and Non-Critical Issues
issue_title: Missing rewardPPQ validation in campaign deployment functions
summary: NudgeCampaignFactory.deployCampaign and deployAndFundCampaign do not validate rewardPPQ. A zero rewardPPQ creates campaigns that distribute no rewards, while rewardPPQ greater than or equal to PPQ_DENOMINATOR can define very high or unsustainable reward rates.
root_cause: missing bounds checks for a core economic reward-rate parameter
why_not_medium_or_high: the issue is primarily bad campaign configuration; without an untrusted setter or concrete drain path, it remains a low/QA economic-parameter risk
operational_risk: campaigns can be economically nonsensical, misleading to participants, or unsustainable for campaign administrators
recommended_mitigation: require rewardPPQ < PPQ_DENOMINATOR or another protocol-defined maximum
when_it_could_escalate: if untrusted users can create funded campaigns with unsafe reward rates
if excessive rewardPPQ enables rapid reward exhaustion under normal participation
if frontends do not surface reward-rate anomalies
